cases.capability: PL/iSQL is installed in the migration database
cases.migration_risk: Oracle-style program units cannot compile without the procedural language.

cases.capability: A PL/iSQL procedure can invoke an orafce function
cases.migration_risk: Extension functions must resolve inside migrated PL/iSQL routines.

cases.capability: ADD_MONTHS preserves end-of-month behavior in a leap year
cases.migration_risk: Billing and maturity calculations frequently depend on month-end rules.

cases.capability: LAST_DAY returns the leap-year month boundary
cases.migration_risk: Period closing logic must preserve leap-day boundaries.

cases.capability: NEXT_DAY advances when the input is already the named weekday
cases.migration_risk: A non-strict weekday calculation shifts schedules by seven days.

cases.capability: MONTHS_BETWEEN treats two month-end dates as a whole month
cases.migration_risk: Fractional-month drift changes accrual and service-period calculations.

cases.capability: TRUNC with IW returns the ISO-week Monday
cases.migration_risk: Reporting windows rely on Oracle ISO-week truncation.

cases.capability: TO_DATE parses an explicit Oracle datetime mask
cases.migration_risk: Explicit Oracle format masks appear in imports and stored routines.

cases.capability: SUBSTR accepts a negative offset from the end
cases.migration_risk: Negative positions are common in identifier and suffix extraction.

cases.capability: INSTR locates a requested occurrence
cases.migration_risk: Occurrence-aware searches cannot be replaced by a simple position call.

cases.capability: LPAD produces fixed-width zero-padded values
cases.migration_risk: Legacy account and document identifiers often require exact padding.

cases.capability: RTRIM removes only the requested trailing character set
cases.migration_risk: Over-trimming changes formatted numeric and business-code values.

cases.capability: REGEXP_INSTR returns Oracle-style one-based position
cases.migration_risk: Regular-expression location semantics affect validation and tokenization.

cases.capability: TO_NUMBER converts a decimal text value
cases.migration_risk: Text-to-number conversions must retain the expected scale.

cases.capability: NVL supplies a fallback for NULL
cases.migration_risk: NVL is pervasive in migrated queries and routines.

cases.capability: NVL2 selects the non-NULL branch
cases.migration_risk: Branch inversion silently corrupts derived flags.

cases.capability: DECODE selects the matching search-result pair
cases.migration_risk: DECODE commonly carries compact business mappings.

cases.capability: LNNVL treats a NULL predicate as true
cases.migration_risk: NULL predicate behavior changes exclusion filters.

cases.capability: NANVL replaces a numeric NaN
cases.migration_risk: Scientific and financial imports may carry NaN sentinel values.

cases.capability: BITAND performs Oracle-compatible integer masking
cases.migration_risk: Packed permission and state flags depend on bit masks.

cases.capability: MOD returns the dividend when the divisor is zero
cases.migration_risk: Oracle MOD zero-divisor behavior differs from naive remainder expressions.

cases.capability: LISTAGG concatenates ordered rows with a delimiter
cases.migration_risk: Denormalized reporting output depends on stable ordered aggregation.

cases.capability: MEDIAN handles an even number of values
cases.migration_risk: Analytical results must retain Oracle median interpolation.

cases.capability: A migrated column retains the oracle.varchar2 type
cases.migration_risk: Falling back to PostgreSQL varchar loses orafce byte semantics.

cases.capability: VARCHAR2 length is measured in UTF-8 bytes
cases.migration_risk: Multibyte data can violate Oracle byte-sized column contracts.

cases.capability: A migrated column retains the oracle.nvarchar2 type
cases.migration_risk: National-character columns require character rather than byte semantics.

cases.capability: NVARCHAR2 stores two multibyte characters in a two-character column
cases.migration_risk: Incorrect semantics reject valid multilingual values or truncate data.

cases.capability: DBMS_ASSERT safely quotes an embedded apostrophe
cases.migration_risk: Dynamic SQL ports need compatible quoting safeguards.

cases.capability: DBMS_ASSERT rejects a non-name SQL fragment
cases.migration_risk: Permissive identifier validation creates an injection path.

cases.capability: DBMS_OUTPUT buffers and retrieves ordered lines
cases.migration_risk: Diagnostic output from migrated routines must remain observable.

cases.capability: DBMS_RANDOM.VALUE respects its half-open range
cases.migration_risk: Out-of-range random values can violate sampling and key-generation logic.

cases.capability: Oracle DUAL exposes exactly one row
cases.migration_risk: Unchanged Oracle queries frequently retain FROM DUAL.

cases.capability: USER_TABLES exposes a migrated application table
cases.migration_risk: Administrative routines inspect Oracle dictionary views.

cases.capability: USER_TAB_COLUMNS exposes all application columns
cases.migration_risk: Metadata-driven applications rely on accurate column discovery.

cases.capability: PRODUCT_COMPONENT_VERSION reports orafce
cases.migration_risk: Compatibility checks use product component metadata.

cases.capability: The compatibility trigger converts an empty string to NULL
cases.migration_risk: Oracle treats empty text as NULL while PostgreSQL normally does not.
